memes.label: "The Mill on the Floss"
memes.description: "“In their death they were not divided.”"

memes.label: "Basset"
memes.description: "Basset is the rural hamlet where Mrs Tulliver's sister, Mrs Moss, lives with her family — a place of deep-rutted lanes, humble farming, and the Tullivers' recurring visits of duty and kinship."

memes.label: "Bob Jakin"
memes.description: "Tom Tulliver's childhood friend who becomes his loyal business partner — representing a warmer, more flexible masculinity than Tom's rigid self-discipline"

memes.label: "Bob Jakin's Flood Rescue"
memes.description: "Bob Jakin rows through the raging flood to rescue Maggie and Tom Tulliver — the novel's final act of loyalty and courage before the tragic ending"
aliases: Bob Jakin rows through the flood

memes.label: "Character Map of The Mill on the Floss"
memes.description: "A relational map of the major characters in George Eliot's The Mill on the Floss, organized by family and social connection"

memes.label: "Characters of The Mill on the Floss"
memes.description: "Complete index of characters in George Eliot's The Mill on the Floss, organized by family, role, and prominence"

memes.label: "Childhood"
memes.description: "The formative experiences of Maggie and Tom Tulliver — childhood as the crucible of character, marked by intense feeling and the seeds of adult tragedy"

memes.label: "Custom"
memes.description: "The unwritten code of traditional behavior that governs provincial life — the Dodson standard of propriety that both sustains community and stifles individuality"

memes.label: The Dodson Family
memes.description: The matriarchal clan whose code of respectability, propriety, and material prudence governs St. Ogg's social order and provides the novel's central moral counterpoint to the Tullivers' passionate impulsiveness.

memes.label: "The Dodson Family Council"
memes.description: "The Dodson aunts and uncles convene at Dorlcote Mill to deliberate the Tulliver bankruptcy — a masterly comic scene that reveals the Dodson code of family duty, respectability, and conditional kindness"

memes.label: "Dorlcote Mill"
memes.description: "The Tulliver family mill on the River Floss — the novel's central location, symbol of family identity, and site of the story's beginning and end"

memes.label: "Dr. Kenn"
memes.description: "The wise, compassionate clergyman of St. Ogg's who offers Maggie refuge and counsel when the community condemns her after the boat incident with Stephen Guest"

memes.label: Dunlow
memes.description: A rural area outside St. Ogg's, comprising Dunlow Common and Dunlow Lane — associated with the gypsy encampment that fascinates the young Maggie Tulliver.

memes.label: Dunlow Common
memes.description: The open common land on the outskirts of St. Ogg's where gypsies encamped, and the destination of Maggie Tulliver's childhood flight from home.

memes.label: "Duty"
memes.description: "Moral obligation and social responsibility as a governing force in the lives of the Tullivers and Dodsons, often in tension with personal desire"

memes.label: "Education"
memes.description: "Formal and informal education as a marker of class, gender, and character — from Tom's Latin at Mr. Stelling's to Maggie's voracious self-directed reading"

memes.label: "Events of The Mill on the Floss"
memes.description: "Complete index of major narrative events in George Eliot's The Mill on the Floss, organized by type and book"

memes.label: "Family"
memes.description: "Central social structure and moral framework in The Mill on the Floss, shaping identity, duty, and conflict across the Tulliver and Dodson clans"

memes.label: "Family Pride"
memes.description: "The driving force of Tulliver and Dodson family identity — both a source of strength and a cause of tragedy in The Mill on the Floss."

memes.label: "Garum Firs"
memes.description: "The home of Mr. and Mrs. Glegg — a house of solid respectability that embodies Dodson values of propriety, thrift, and careful management"

memes.label: "Gypsies in The Mill on the Floss"
memes.description: "The gypsy community in The Mill on the Floss as a symbol of freedom, otherness, and the imaginative escape that Maggie Tulliver seeks from provincial constraint."

memes.label: "Imagination"
memes.description: "Imagination as the shaping faculty of inner life in The Mill on the Floss — Maggie's vital gift that is also her vulnerability, contrasted with Tom's practical limitations of vision"

memes.label: Jetsome
memes.description: The young man hired by Lawyer Wakem to manage Dorlcote Mill after Mr. Tulliver's downfall, whose incompetence and loose character disappoint Wakem.

memes.label: "Justice"
memes.description: "Legal and moral justice as competing frameworks — the lawsuit that ruins the Tullivers, the bankruptcy that reshapes their world, and the limits of law as a moral instrument"

memes.label: "Kezia"
memes.description: "Kezia is the Tulliver family's good-hearted, bad-tempered housemaid, a loyal domestic presence who mediates between the kitchen, the parlour, and the children's emotional storms."

memes.label: Laceham
memes.description: An ancient market town on the River Floss, known for its quality linen goods and as the destination of Bob Jakin's first independent trading journey.

memes.label: "Lawyer Wakem"
memes.description: "The lawyer whose victory over Mr. Tulliver in a water-rights lawsuit makes him the Tulliver family's sworn enemy — yet he is revealed to be a devoted father to his disabled son Philip"

memes.label: "Love"
memes.description: "The powerful emotional force that binds and divides characters — sibling love, romantic passion, and the yearning for belonging in The Mill on the Floss"

memes.label: "Lucy Deane"
memes.description: "Lucy Deane is Maggie Tulliver's fair-haired cousin, whose gentle goodness, beauty, and trusting nature make her the innocent victim of the novel's central romantic entanglement."

memes.label: "Luke"
memes.description: "The loyal head miller at Dorlcote Mill whose steadfast service and simple goodness represent the dignity of working-class life"

memes.label: "Maggie and Philip in the Red Deeps"
memes.description: "Maggie Tulliver's secret meetings with Philip Wakem in the Red Deeps — a friendship that crosses the barrier of family hatred and becomes Maggie's first experience of intellectual companionship"

memes.label: "Maggie Runs Away to the Gypsies"
memes.description: "Young Maggie Tulliver runs away to a gypsy encampment on the common — a childhood rebellion that reveals her longing for a world beyond the constraints of Dorlcote Mill and Dodson respectability"

memes.label: "Maggie's Return to St Ogg's"
memes.description: "Maggie returns alone to St Ogg's after her boat trip with Stephen Guest, facing devastating social ostracism and moral condemnation from the community"

memes.label: "Maggie Tulliver"
memes.description: "The passionate, intelligent protagonist of The Mill on the Floss, whose hunger for love and knowledge conflicts with the constraints of Victorian provincial womanhood"

memes.label: "Memory"
memes.description: "The persistent presence of the past in the present — how memory shapes identity, binds characters to childhood, and drives the novel's elegiac tone"

memes.label: "Miss Clint"
memes.description: "Miss Clint became Mrs. Wakem, the wife of Lawyer Wakem — a marriage Mrs. Tulliver recalls from her youth as evidence that Wakem was once an ordinary, unremarkable man."

memes.label: "The Miss Dodsons"
memes.description: "The Miss Dodsons were the four Dodson sisters — Mrs. Glegg, Mrs. Pullet, Mrs. Deane, and Mrs. Tulliver — known before their marriages for their respectability and status in provincial society."

memes.label: "Miss Firniss"
memes.description: "Headmistress of the boarding school in Laceham where Maggie Tulliver and her cousin Lucy Deane are sent for their education"

memes.label: "The Miss Guests"
memes.description: "The Miss Guests are the fashionable sisters of Stephen Guest, leaders of St Ogg's society who view Maggie with suspicion and condescension."

memes.label: "Mr. Askern"
memes.description: "Surgeon attending Tom Tulliver after his foot injury at King's Lorton, mentioned during the period of Tom's convalescence and Philip Wakem's kindness"

memes.label: "Mr. Carr"
memes.description: "Mr. Carr was a man known to the Dodson family who became 'childish' after illness — a cautionary example invoked by Mrs. Pullet during Mr. Tulliver's sickness."

memes.label: "Mr. Deane"
memes.description: "Manager at Guest & Co, husband of Mrs. Deane, and father of Lucy Deane — a successful, prudent businessman who embodies Dodson pragmatism."

memes.label: "Mr. Glegg"
memes.description: "Retired wool-stapler and husband of Mrs. Glegg, a kind-hearted man whose gentle nature contrasts with his wife's severity."
aliases: Mr. Glegg of the Dodson family

memes.label: "Mr. Goodrich"
memes.description: "Drawing master at King's Lorton, engaged by Mr. Stelling to teach Tom Tulliver landscape drawing."

memes.label: "Mr. Gore"
memes.description: "Mr. Gore is Mr. Tulliver's attorney in St Ogg's, who handles the aftermath of the lost lawsuit and delivers the devastating news that Wakem has acquired the mortgage on Dorlcote Mill."

memes.label: "Mr. Guest"
memes.description: "Mr. Guest is the senior partner of Guest & Co, a shipping and trading firm in St Ogg's, and the father of Stephen Guest."

memes.label: "Mr. Hyndmarsh"
memes.description: "Grocer in St Ogg's, on Danish Street, purchaser of Mrs. Tulliver's pickles and ketchup."

memes.label: "Mr. Jacobs"
memes.description: "Tom Tulliver's first schoolmaster, familiarly known as \"Old Goggles\" for his spectacles, whose inadequate academy prompts Mr. Tulliver to send Tom to Mr. Stelling"

memes.label: "Mr. Moss"
memes.description: "Mr. Moss is the husband of Gritty Moss (née Tulliver), a struggling farmer at Basset, and brother-in-law to Mr. Tulliver."

memes.label: "Mr. Pivart"
memes.description: "A wealthy landowner whose upstream irrigation works trigger the water-rights lawsuit that leads to Mr. Tulliver's financial ruin."

memes.label: "Mr. Poulter"
memes.description: "Mr. Poulter is the village schoolmaster at King's Lorton who, as a veteran of the Peninsular War, drills Tom Tulliver and inadvertently provides him with the sword that causes his injury."

memes.label: "Mr. Pullet"
memes.description: "Gentleman farmer and husband of Mrs. Pullet, a confused but good-natured man who defers to his wife in all matters of consequence."

memes.label: "Mr. Riley"
memes.description: "The auctioneer and appraiser who advises Mr. Tulliver on Tom's education — a minor character whose bad advice has major consequences"

memes.label: "Mr. Spence"
memes.description: "Managing clerk at Guest & Co in St Ogg's, present during Tom Tulliver's interview with Mr. Deane."

memes.label: "Mr. Spray"
memes.description: "Independent minister in St Ogg's whose political sermons on the Catholic Question unsettle the town's religious and social equilibrium"

memes.label: "Mr. Stelling"
memes.description: "The clergyman-schoolmaster who educates Tom Tulliver — a portrait of institutional pedagogy that serves the system rather than the student"

memes.label: "Mr. Tulliver"
memes.description: "The proud, impulsive miller of Dorlcote Mill whose disastrous lawsuit against Lawyer Wakem plunges the Tulliver family into bankruptcy and sets the novel's tragic plot in motion"

memes.label: "Death of Mr. Tulliver"
memes.description: "The death of Mr. Tulliver after he attacks Lawyer Wakem — a violent, pathetic end that passes the burden of revenge to his son Tom"

memes.label: "Mr. Turnbull"
memes.description: "Mr. Turnbull is a medical practitioner who attends Mr. Tulliver during his final illness and informs the family of the severity of his condition."

memes.label: "Mrs. Deane"
memes.description: "The most prosperous and worldly of the Dodson sisters, mother of Lucy Deane, whose social elevation marks the family's aspirational trajectory"

memes.label: "Mrs. Glegg"
memes.description: "The eldest Dodson sister, whose rigid adherence to family custom and sharp tongue make her the novel's most formidable matriarch and its most memorable comic creation"

memes.label: "Mrs. Jakin"
memes.description: "Mrs. Jakin is Bob Jakin's mother, a widow who keeps lodgings in St Ogg's and provides shelter to Maggie Tulliver in her final days."

memes.label: "Mrs. Kenn"
memes.description: "Mrs. Kenn is the deceased wife of Dr. Kenn, the clergyman of St Ogg's, whose death several years earlier leaves him a widower and ultimately contributes to the scandal surrounding his association with Maggie Tulliver."

memes.label: "Mrs. Moss"
memes.description: "Mr. Tulliver's sister Gritty, who married Mr. Moss, a poor farmer; she lives in hardship at Basset with her eight children."

memes.label: "Mrs. Pullet"
memes.description: "The most sentimental and hypochondriacal of the Dodson sisters, whose elaborate mourning and obsession with illness provide the novel's richest comic passages"

memes.label: "Mrs. Sutton"
memes.description: "Mrs. Sutton is a deceased wealthy parishioner of St Ogg's, whose will and the disposition of her fortune become a topic of conversation among the Dodson aunts."

memes.label: "Mrs. Tulliver"
memes.description: "Maggie and Tom's mother, a Dodson by birth whose domestic helplessness and obsession with household goods make her both comic and pitiable"

memes.label: "Mrs. Wooll"
memes.description: "Mrs. Wooll of St Ogg's is a lace-buying acquaintance mentioned in Dodson family conversation, serving as a social benchmark for conspicuous consumption in provincial society."

memes.label: "Mudport"
memes.description: "The nearby port town where Bob Jakin establishes himself as a trader — representing the commercial world beyond St. Ogg's provincial boundaries"

memes.label: "Music"
memes.description: "Music as emotional expression and social performance — from Maggie's passionate response to music to the drawing-room performances that mark class and cultivation"

memes.label: "Nature"
memes.description: "The natural world — especially the River Floss — as setting, symbol, and moral presence in Eliot's novel, reflecting and shaping human destiny"

memes.label: Park House
memes.description: The grand residence of the Deane family, elevated above the flood-plain — a symbol of Dodson prosperity and the setting for the novel's culminating social events.

memes.label: "Philip Wakem"
memes.description: "The intelligent, sensitive, hunchbacked son of Lawyer Wakem — Tom's schoolfellow, Maggie's intellectual companion, and the forbidden love that tests family loyalty"

memes.label: "Places of The Mill on the Floss"
memes.description: "Complete index of places and locations in George Eliot's The Mill on the Floss, organized by type and significance"

memes.label: "Pride"
memes.description: "Family pride as both social identity and moral failing — the Dodsons' defining trait and the source of their limitations in The Mill on the Floss"

memes.label: "Property"
memes.description: "Property as the material basis of social identity — the mill, land, inheritance, and debt that determine the Tullivers' fate and drive the novel's economic plot"

memes.label: "Provincial Life"
memes.description: "Provincial life as a social system in The Mill on the Floss — the codes, hierarchies, and rhythms of a mid-nineteenth-century English country town."

memes.label: "Religion"
memes.description: "Religious belief and practice in provincial England, from Dodson semi-paganism to Maggie's ascetic Christianity derived from Thomas à Kempis"

memes.label: "Renunciation"
memes.description: "Maggie Tulliver's adopted doctrine of self-denial, drawn from Thomas à Kempis, as both spiritual discipline and psychological escape from desire"

memes.label: The Round Pool
memes.description: A deep pool in the river near Dorlcote Mill where Tom and Maggie Tulliver fished as children — a site of childhood adventure, sibling bonding, and flood-myth.

memes.label: "Ruin and Bankruptcy"
memes.description: "Mr. Tulliver's financial ruin and its cascading consequences for the Tulliver family, the novel's central economic catastrophe."

memes.label: "Sacrifice"
memes.description: "Sacrifice is a central moral concept in The Mill on the Floss — the demand that individual desire yield to the claims of family, duty, and love, embodied most fully in Maggie Tulliver's repeated acts of renunciation."

memes.label: "Sally"
memes.description: "Sally is the Tullivers' maid at Dorlcote Mill, a minor domestic character who appears in several early chapters."

memes.label: "Shame"
memes.description: "Shame is a pervasive social and psychological force in The Mill on the Floss — the engine of provincial moral judgment, the sting of fallen status, and the internalised voice that shapes Maggie Tulliver's conscience."

memes.label: "Sir John Crake"
memes.description: "Master of the harriers, a local gentleman whom young Tom Tulliver admires and wishes to resemble, representing the provincial ideal of gentlemanly accomplishment"

memes.label: "Squire Darleigh"
memes.description: "Squire Darleigh is a local landowner near St Ogg's who hosted dances in the Dodsons' youth, known as a social figure in the provincial gentry."

memes.label: St. Ogg's
memes.description: Redirect to st-oggs-town — the provincial market town that is the novel's social world.

memes.label: "St. Ogg's"
memes.description: "The provincial market town that is the novel's social world — a community governed by custom, gossip, and the rigid morality that ultimately destroys Maggie"

memes.label: "Stephen Guest"
memes.description: "Lucy Deane's handsome, wealthy suitor whose passionate attraction to Maggie precipitates the novel's central moral crisis and Maggie's social downfall"

memes.label: "Stephen Guest Courts Maggie"
memes.description: "Stephen Guest's pursuit of Maggie Tulliver while engaged to her cousin Lucy Deane — a romantic entanglement that tests Maggie's moral resolve and precipitates the novel's climactic crisis"

memes.label: "Sympathy"
memes.description: "Eliot's central moral value — the capacity to feel with others — presented as the necessary counterweight to rigid duty and social judgment"

memes.label: "The Auction"
memes.description: "The public auction of the Tullivers' household goods following Mr. Tulliver's bankruptcy — a scene of public humiliation and communal spectacle"

memes.label: "The Flood"
memes.description: "The climactic flood of the River Floss that drowns Maggie and Tom Tulliver together — the novel's tragic resolution, reuniting the siblings in death"

memes.label: "The River Floss"
memes.description: "The river that flows through the novel's landscape — source of the mill's power, Maggie's childhood playground, and the instrument of the story's tragic conclusion"

memes.label: "The Lawsuit"
memes.description: "Mr. Tulliver's disastrous legal action against Lawyer Wakem over water rights on the Floss — the event that triggers the family's financial ruin and establishes the central conflict"

memes.label: "The Mill on the Floss (Title Entity)"
memes.description: "The Mill on the Floss as title entity — Dorlcote Mill on the River Floss, the novel's central symbol of home, livelihood, loss, and the inexorable current of fate."

memes.label: "The Near-Elopement"
memes.description: "Maggie's near-elopement with Stephen Guest — the novel's central moral crisis, in which Maggie chooses duty over desire but is condemned by society nonetheless"

memes.label: "The Quarrel Between Maggie and Tom"
memes.description: "The bitter rift between Maggie and Tom Tulliver after her return from the near-elopement — a moral confrontation that crystallises the novel's central conflict between legalistic duty and compassionate understanding."

memes.label: "The Red Deeps"
memes.description: "A secluded hollow in the landscape near Dorlcote where Maggie and Philip Wakem meet in secret — a pastoral space of intimacy forbidden by family loyalty"

memes.label: "The Ripple"
memes.description: "The Ripple is the small tributary stream that flows past Dorlcote Mill into the River Floss — a place of childhood wonder, natural beauty, and, in the novel's climactic flood, deadly force."

memes.label: "Themes of The Mill on the Floss"
memes.description: "A dynamically generated index of major themes, concepts, and motifs in George Eliot's The Mill on the Floss"

memes.label: "Narrative Timeline of The Mill on the Floss"
memes.description: "A book-by-book narrative timeline of George Eliot's The Mill on the Floss, charting the Tulliver family's story from childhood to the final flood"

memes.label: Tofton
memes.description: A village on the River Floss downstream from St. Ogg's, with a ferry crossing and fine houses among trees — the setting of the novel's climactic flood scene.

memes.label: "Tom's Education at Mr Stelling's"
memes.description: "Tom Tulliver's classical education under the Reverend Walter Stelling at King's Lorton — a misdirected investment that exemplifies the novel's critique of a gentleman's education imposed on a boy destined for practical life."

memes.label: "Tom Tulliver"
memes.description: "Maggie's pragmatic, duty-driven older brother, whose narrow but genuine sense of responsibility propels the novel's economic plot and precipitates its tragic conclusion"

memes.label: "Tom's Redemption"
memes.description: "Tom Tulliver's long campaign to repay his father's creditors and regain Dorlcote Mill — a triumph of filial duty that comes at the cost of emotional narrowness"

memes.label: "The Tulliver Family"
memes.description: "The Tulliver family — Mr. and Mrs. Tulliver and their children Tom and Maggie — the novel's central domestic unit whose rise and fall drives the narrative."

memes.label: "Woman"
memes.description: "The constrained social position of women in provincial Victorian England — Maggie's struggle against gendered expectations that limit education, autonomy, and moral agency"
